Chongqing · Travel Guide
Group 6–7 Summer 4 Days
The Mountain City

Chongqing
in Four Days

Neon cliffs over rivers, monorails through apartment buildings, UNESCO karst canyons, and hotpot in 40°C heat. Chongqing is unlike anywhere on earth.

Day 01Arrive, Explore & Feel the City

🏙️
Jiefangbei CBD
Base Camp · Stay Here
Jiefangbei CBD 解放碑

Stay in the central business district — underground malls, elevated walkways, and street food stalls layered on top of each other. The Liberation Monument stands at the center; everything is within walking distance. Drop your bags and head straight out.

Getting Here from Airport
🚇
Metro Line 10 (Airport) → transfer → Line 1 to Xiaoshizi station~55 min · ¥8
🚖
Didi from CKG airport — best for group with bags~45 min · ¥80–100
↗ Google Maps Best central location
🏘️
Ciqikou Ancient Town
Heritage · Ming Dynasty
Ciqikou Ancient Town 磁器口

A Ming Dynasty river-town that survived modernization — stone alleyways, tea houses, temples, and craft vendors. Arrive early afternoon before the biggest tourist crowds. Wander the maze of side lanes beyond the main street for the authentic feel. Must-eat here: Mao Xuewang (毛血旺) from a tiny street stall and hemp-spiced duck jaw (麻辣鸭脖) from vendors.

How to Get There
🚇
Metro Line 1 → Ciqikou station, 5 min walk to entrance~25 min from Jiefangbei
🚖
Didi from hotel~20 min · ¥25–35
↗ Google Maps Explore side lanes beyond main street
🛕
Luohan Temple
Temple · Historic · Active
Luohan Temple 罗汉寺

A functioning Buddhist temple tucked into downtown Chongqing, dating back to the Song Dynasty. Famous for its collection of over 500 luohan (arhat) clay statues — each with a unique expression, lined in long dimly-lit halls. The temple is genuinely active, with monks, incense smoke, and worshippers creating an atmosphere completely at odds with the surrounding city. Entrance is free or very cheap.

How to Get There
🚶
Walk from Jiefangbei CBD — it's hidden down a small lane near Minzu Road~10 min on foot
🚇
Metro Line 1 or 6 → Xiaoshizi, short walk south~5 min walk
↗ Google Maps Free entry · dress modestly
🌆
Hongya Cave
Landmark · Evening Icon
Hongya Cave 洪崖洞

The 11-story cliffside building that defines Chongqing's image — best at dusk (6:30–7pm) as it blazes neon over the Jialing River. Don't eat inside (tourist-priced) — just explore and photograph. Then walk the Binjiang Road riverside promenade toward Chaotianmen, the confluence of the Yangtze and Jialing rivers. At night with lit bridges and passing ferries, it's genuinely cinematic.

How to Get There
🚶
Walk from Jiefangbei CBD or Luohan Temple~10–15 min on foot
🚇
Metro Line 1 → Xiaoshizi, then walk north along riverside~8 min walk
↗ Google Maps Go at 6:30pm for the neon glow
🔥
Liu Yishou Hotpot
Dinner · Must-Do
Liu Yishou Hotpot 刘一手火锅

Local chain beloved by Chongqing residents, not a tourist trap. Order the split pot (half spicy / half mild). Must-orders: beef tripe (毛肚), duck intestines (鸭肠), fatty beef (肥牛), lotus root, tofu skin. The sesame dipping sauce is everything. Hotpot in 35°C heat is a Chongqing tradition — lean in.

How to Get There
🚶
Walk from Hongya Cave area~10–15 min
🚖
Didi — search "刘一手火锅解放碑" in app~¥12–18
↗ Google Maps Book ahead for 6–7 pax
Day 1 food highlights
毛血旺 Offal in spicy broth (Ciqikou)
麻辣鸭脖 Spiced duck jaw (Ciqikou)
毛肚 Beef tripe (hotpot)
鸭肠 Duck intestines (hotpot)
肥牛 Fatty beef (hotpot)
芝麻酱 Sesame dip
Day 02City Scenes, Streets & Skyline
Morning → Late Evening

🐼
Chongqing Zoo
Wildlife · Giant Pandas
Chongqing Zoo 重庆动物园

One of the best places in China to see giant pandas up close without the crowds of Chengdu's research base. The zoo's panda enclosures let you get genuinely close to these animals in a relaxed setting. Arrive when they open at 8:30am — pandas are most active in the cool morning. The zoo also houses red pandas, golden monkeys, and giraffes. Allow 2–2.5 hours.

How to Get There
🚇
Metro Line 2 → Dongyuan station, 10 min walk~25 min from Jiefangbei
🚖
Didi from hotel~20 min · ¥20–30
↗ Google Maps Go early — pandas active at 9am
🚝
Liziba Monorail
Icon · Unique Transit
Liziba Station — train through a building

Metro Line 2 passes directly through a residential apartment building on floors 6–8. Locals just living their lives while a monorail glides past their windows. Ride it, then cross to the observation platform for the exterior shot. Perfectly placed between the zoo and Daijia Alley.

How to Get There
🚇
Metro Line 2 — ride from Chongqing Zoo area (Dongyuan) → Liziba~3 stops · 8 min
📸
Exit B, cross road for observation deck view of train entering building
↗ Google Maps Exit B → cross road for photo
🏮
Daijia Alley
Heritage · Art Street
Daijia Alley 戴家巷

A revitalized heritage alley carved into the cliff face above the Jialing River — one of the most photogenic spots in Chongqing. Colourful stairway murals, independent cafés, small studios, and pop-up vendors line the narrow lane. The cliff-edge viewpoints give dramatic perspectives of the river below and the city's layers stacked up above. Best in the afternoon light.

How to Get There
🚖
Didi from Liziba~10 min · ¥15–20
🚶
Walk downhill from Eling area toward the riverfront cliff path~15 min walk
↗ Google Maps Best for photos in afternoon light
🌉
Qiansimen Bridge
Viewpoint · Bridge Walk
Qiansimen Bridge 千厮门大桥

Walking across this cable-stayed bridge gives you the definitive money-shot of Hongya Cave — the full 11-story cliffside building framed in the distance, reflected in the Jialing River. This is the angle everyone is looking for but can't find from the ground. Walk the full span across and back; the views change dramatically as you move. Best in late afternoon or at dusk.

How to Get There
🚶
Walk from Daijia Alley down to the riverfront, then along to bridge entrance~10 min
🚖
Didi to Qiansimen Bridge north entrance~8 min · ¥12
↗ Google Maps Best Hongya Cave photo angle
🪜
Xiahaoli Old Street
Historic · Hidden Gem
Xiahaoli Old Street 下浩里老街

A crumbling, beautiful old street on the south bank that feels completely authentic — unlike the polished tourist alleys elsewhere. Narrow stone stairways, old shophouses, local barbers, and mahjong dens. The street runs down the hillside toward the Yangtze with cinematic views at the bottom. A handful of cool independent coffee shops and bars have opened here, drawing a young local crowd in the evenings.

How to Get There
🚖
Didi from Qiansimen Bridge to south bank~15 min · ¥18–25
🚇
Metro Line 6 → Luohan Temple station (south bank), 15 min walk~25 min total
↗ Google Maps Real local vibe — not tourist
🌅
Luori Ye Cha Rooftop
Rooftop Bar · Sunset
Luori Ye Cha 落日野茶

One of Chongqing's most talked-about rooftop bars — the name translates to "Sunset Wild Tea." Perched on a high building with sweeping panoramic views of the city and rivers, it's best visited at sunset for that signature golden light over Chongqing's stacked skyline. Expect cocktails with Chinese botanicals, artisanal teas, and a crowd that actually appreciates the view. Book or arrive early — it fills up fast on good-weather evenings.

How to Get There
🚖
Didi from Xiahaoli area — search "落日野茶" in the app~10–15 min
📍
Confirm exact location on WeChat or Xiaohongshu (小红书) before going — venues like this sometimes move
↗ Google Maps Arrive 30 min before sunset
Day 2 eats & drinks
小面 Spicy noodles — lunch ¥15
糍粑 Sticky rice cake (street snack)
酸辣粉 Sour-spicy glass noodles
特调茶饮 Craft tea cocktails (Luori)
冰粉 Cold jelly dessert
Day 03Wulong Day Trip — Trip Highlight

🚐
Van to Wulong
Transport · 2.5 hrs
Drive to Wulong Karst Park 武隆喀斯特

UNESCO World Heritage Site, 2.5 hours from central Chongqing. Leave by 7am to beat the heat and midday crowds. Bring water, sunscreen, and closed-toe shoes. The landscape looks genuinely alien.

Getting to Wulong — 3 Options
🚐
Private van (recommended for group) — hire from hotel concierge night before2.5 hr · ¥600–800 RT
🚄
High-speed train Chongqing North → Wulong station, then local bus to park~1.5 hr train + 40 min bus
🚌
Long-distance bus from Caiyuanba bus terminal~3 hr · ¥60/person
↗ Google Maps Van best for 6–7 people
🕳️
Tiankeng Sinkholes
UNESCO · Karst Geology
Tiankeng Difeng 天坑地缝

Three massive karst sinkholes the size of stadiums connected by underground rivers and sheer cliff paths. Walk the canyon floor between them. Scale is impossible to convey in photos — the walls tower hundreds of meters above you.

Inside Wulong Park
🎟️
Park shuttle bus included with entrance ticket between scenic areas
🥾
Walk between sinkhole viewpoints once inside~2 km of paths
↗ Google Maps
🌉
★ Three Natural Bridges
★ Trip Highlight · UNESCO
Three Natural Bridges 天生三桥

Three colossal natural stone arches stacked in sequence over a gorge — filming location for Transformers: Age of Extinction and Mission Impossible. The scale defies comprehension until you stand inside one, looking up at 200+ meters of rock overhead. Allow 2–3 hours for the full walk.

Getting Around Inside
🎟️
Park shuttle from ticket gate to trailhead (included in ticket)
🥾
Self-guided walk ~4 km through all three arches and gorge floor2–3 hrs
⬆️
Elevator/lift available at exit point for those unable to climb stairs
↗ Google Maps 2–3hr walk · closed-toe shoes
💎
Furong Cave — optional
Optional Add-On
Furong Cave 芙蓉洞

Vast illuminated stalactite cave system — spectacular formations. Add this only if energy and time allow. The bridges walk is 4–6km total and the heat takes a toll. Skip if the group is tired.

Getting There
🚌
Local bus or taxi from Three Bridges scenic area~20 min · ¥15–30
↗ Google Maps Only if energy allows
🐟
Grilled Fish · Nanbin Rd
Dinner · Back in City
Chongqing Grilled Fish 烤鱼 — Nanbin Road

Back in Chongqing by 7pm. Fish on an iron plate surrounded by chilies, pickled vegetables and herbs. Order cold Chongqing beers. Nanbin Road has the best evening skyline view back at the city.

Wulong → Nanbin Road
🚐
Same private van drops you back in the city center~2.5 hr return
🚖
Didi from Jiefangbei to Nanbin Road~15 min · ¥20
↗ Nanbin Road Maps
Day 3 Essentials
Leave by 7am — heat and crowds build fast after 11am
Pack: water, sunscreen, closed-toe shoes — 4–6km walking total
Book private van the night before through hotel concierge
Park ticket covers shuttle buses between scenic areas
Day 04Vertical City, History & Depart
Morning → Airport CKG
9 AM
Liziba & Raffles
11 AM
Guild hall
1 PM
Free museum
4 PM
Depart
🌁
Liziba & Raffles
Vertical City · Morning
Liziba Monorail + Raffles Crystal Link 李子坝 · 来福士

Start the morning with Chongqing's two most iconic vertical experiences. First: Metro Line 2 through the apartment building at Liziba Station — ride it and photograph from the street-level observation point. Then walk or Didi to Raffles City for the Crystal Link sky bridge at 250m, with vertigo-inducing views of both rivers below. Morning light makes the confluence especially beautiful.

How to Get There
🚇
Metro Line 2 from hotel → Liziba Station~20 min
🚶
Then walk / Didi from Liziba to Raffles City (Chaotianmen)~10 min ride or 25 min walk
↗ Liziba Maps ↗ Raffles Maps
🏯
Huguang Guild Hall
History · Rarely Crowded
Huguang Guild Hall 湖广会馆

Stunning Qing Dynasty complex (1759) overlooking the Yangtze. Intricate wood carvings, courtyards, museum about river migration history. Rarely crowded. Traditional Chinese opera performances available via booking.

How to Get There
🚖
Didi from Raffles City / Chaotianmen~8 min · ¥12–18
🚶
Walk along the Yangtze riverfront from Chaotianmen~15 min scenic walk
↗ Google Maps
🏛️
Three Gorges Museum
Museum · FREE · AC
Three Gorges Museum 三峡博物馆

Massive, free, air-conditioned — perfect midday heat escape. The WWII Chongqing resistance exhibition is especially moving: the city was China's wartime capital, bombed heavily by Japan. Give it 2 hours. Next to People's Square.

How to Get There
🚇
Metro Line 1 or 2 → Linjiangmen or People's Square station~20 min from Huguang
🚖
Didi from Huguang Guild Hall~12 min · ¥15–20
↗ Google Maps Free · allow 2 hours
✈️
Jiangbei Airport
Depart
Jiangbei Airport 重庆江北机场 · CKG

Grab Jiefangbei souvenirs before heading out: hotpot spice bases (火锅底料), Sichuan peppercorn (花椒), local baijiu (白酒), handmade lacquerware from Ciqikou shops. Allow 2.5 hours before flight time.

Getting to CKG Airport
🚇
Metro Line 10 from any central station → Jiangbei Airport T2/T3~45 min · ¥8
🚖
Didi van — best for group with luggage, request "大型车"~40 min · ¥80–100
⏰
Allow 2.5 hrs before flight departure — international security is thorough
↗ Google Maps Leave hotel by 4pm latest
Day 4 bites & souvenirs
小面 Spicy noodles (breakfast)
火锅底料 Hotpot base (souvenir)
花椒 Sichuan peppercorn
白酒 Local baijiu
漆器 Lacquerware

Getting Around Chongqing

Complete transport guide — metro lines, Didi tips & day-by-day routes

Daily Transport
🚇 Metro Network
1
Line 1 — main spine (Jiefangbei, Ciqikou, Shapingba)
¥2–6 per trip · runs 6:30am–11pm
2
Line 2 — passes through Liziba building, connects to Jiaochangkou
Same fares as above
6
Line 6 — Chaotianmen (Raffles City) ↔ North Railway Station
Key line for east–west travel
10
Line 10 — Airport Express to CKG T2 & T3
45 min · ¥8 from city center
On-Demand Rides
🚖 Didi (Chinese Uber)
①
Download Didi app and link international card or WeChat Pay before arrival
②
For group of 6–7, select "大型车" (large vehicle/van) in app
Seats 6–7 with luggage · ¥40–100 depending on route
③
Day 3: negotiate a full-day private hire for Wulong (~¥600–800)
Ask hotel concierge night before — they have trusted drivers
Day 1 Route
🌆 Airport → Ciqikou → Hongya
A
CKG Airport → Metro Line 10 → transfer → Line 1 → Xiaoshizi (Jiefangbei hotel)
~55 min · ¥8 per person
B
Metro Line 1 → Ciqikou station, explore old town, then Didi to Luohan Temple
~25 min metro + ¥15 Didi
C
Walk from Luohan Temple → Hongya Cave (10 min) → riverside promenade → Didi to hotpot
Free walk · Didi ¥12–18
Day 3 Route
🏔️ City → Wulong → City
A
7am: Private van picks up from hotel → Wulong park entrance
~2.5 hrs · arrange night before
B
Park shuttle buses between Tiankeng, Three Bridges (included in ticket)
Park ticket ~¥135/person
C
5pm: Van returns to Chongqing → drop at Nanbin Road for dinner
Back by ~7:30pm
Day 4 Route
🏛️ Vertical City Loop → CKG
A
Hotel → Metro Line 2 → Liziba Station (monorail through building)
~20 min · ¥4
B
Didi: Liziba → Xiahaoli → Huguang Guild Hall → Three Gorges Museum
~¥12–18 each leg
C
Museum → hotel (collect bags) → Metro Line 10 to CKG Airport
Leave by 4pm for evening flight · Line 10 takes 45 min
Payment & Apps
📱 Essential Setup
①
WeChat Pay or Alipay — essential for Didi, metro, restaurants, street food
Link international card before trip
②
Download Didi app + set language to English
Works with Google Maps for addresses
③
Chongqing Metro app (重庆轨道) for real-time routes
Or use Baidu Maps / Apple Maps offline

Food Cheat Sheet

6 essential dishes
Dish What it is Where to find When
火锅HotpotSpicy communal broth, cook-your-own. Order split pot (half mild) for first-timers.Everywhere — ask locals for non-tourist recsDay 1 dinner
小面Xiaomian noodlesSpicy noodles, the city's soul food. Under ¥15 a bowl.Street shops near hotel, any breakfast spotDay 2 dinner
毛血旺Mao XuewangOffal, tofu and vegetables in blood-red spicy broth. Punchy and polarizing.Ciqikou street stalls, local restaurantsDay 4 morning
烤鱼Grilled fishFish on an iron plate surrounded by chilies, pickled veg and herbs.Nanbin Road riverside restaurantsDay 3 dinner
酸辣粉Glass noodlesTangy, numbing, addictive. The Sichuan peppercorn numbs your tongue — it's a feature.Night markets, street stallsAnytime
糍粑Ci BaSticky glutinous rice cake, grilled or fried. Best late-night snack.Street vendors, CiqikouAnytime

Practical Info

Everything you actually need to know
🌡️
Summer Heat
35–40°C, very humid. All outdoor activities before 11am or after 5pm. Midday = AC museums and malls.
💳
Payment
WeChat Pay or Alipay essential. Most street food won't take cards. Bring ¥2,000–3,000 per person for 4 days.
📱
Apps
Didi, Pleco (dictionary), Google Translate with Chinese offline pack. Most younger locals understand phone translation.
🍽️
Group Dining
For 6–7 people, always book via hotel concierge ahead. Walk-ins at popular spots often wait 1–2 hours.
🗣️
Language
Download Google Translate Chinese offline pack. Learn: 多少钱 (how much?), 不辣 (not spicy), 谢谢 (thank you).
🏥
Health Tips
Stay hydrated — heat is no joke. Wear light breathable clothes. Sunscreen every day. Bring electrolyte tablets.
Chongqing 🌶️
4-Day Itinerary · Group of 6–7
Summer Edition · Chongqing, China
